items.title: Cary council faces first public meeting since audit exposed questionable expenses
items.summary_raw: Cary residents are expected to confront town leaders Thursday during the first regular council meeting since a state audit flagged questionable spending, including tens of thousands of dollars for a ghostwritten book and a “Dancing Queen” documentary.

items.title: Ask the Meteorologist: How can we stay safe when traveling during flash flooding?
items.summary_raw: With multiple flood alerts issued throughout the day Thursday, it's important that we use plenty of caution on the roads. Here are some things to remember before you drive!

items.title: Meta’s New Feel-Good AI Ad Uses a Song About the World Ending
items.summary_raw: The clip features the David Bowie track “Five Years,” which includes lyrics such as “Earth was really dying (dying).”

items.title: Trump to impose double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries
items.summary_raw: The United States will slap taxes of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries accounting for 99% of U.S. imports, charging that they have inadequately enforced bans on goods produced by forced labor.

items.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
items.summary_raw: Sonam Wangchuk, a supporter of youth-led protesters demanding education reforms, says he wants to avert "possible violence".

items.title: Orcas filmed smashing giant sunfish into thousands of pieces
items.summary_raw: Researchers in the Gulf of California have documented a dramatic orca behavior never described before. In two separate encounters, one orca gripped a dead sunfish by the fin while another charged into it at high speed, blasting the carcass into thousands of pieces.

items.title: Common insulin pen faces supply issues amid rising demand
items.summary_raw: Sanofi has confirmed intermittent supply issues affecting Lantus SoloStar insulin pens due to increased demand. Here's what patients should know if they're having trouble filling their prescriptions.

items.summary_raw: Intense heat followed by cool rains can lead to cracks in your car windshield.

items.title: For Taylor Swift, Madison Square Garden’s Controversial Cameras Briefly Went Dark
items.summary_raw: MSG’s sprawling surveillance system can monitor guests down to the second. Its owners made an exception for the pop star’s rehearsal dinner.

items.title: Trump allies are poised to weaken protections for historic sites — not just in D.C.
items.summary_raw: A council packed with Trump appointees is poised to rewrite historic preservation rules. Critics say that could help him build his D.C. arch faster — and endanger important sites across the country.

items.title: How to keep an ancient text interesting to students? Make it a summer blockbuster
items.summary_raw: English teachers are excited to have a new adaptation of Homer's The Odyssey to use to get students excited about the epic poem.

items.title: A healthier global diet could cut farm emissions by 85%
items.summary_raw: A global shift toward healthier diets could sharply reduce livestock farming, free up agricultural land, and cut emissions from land-use change by 85% by 2050. At the same time, fruit, vegetable, nut, and legume production could soar, creating a sweeping transformation of what the world grows and eats.

items.summary_raw: Piedmont Natural Gas said crews were able to safely extinguish the line before any further damage was done.

items.summary_raw: The European Commission claims that Google boosted its own apps and products to the top of search rankings to the detriment of its competitors.

items.title: For a 2nd time, House approves resolution to end the war in Iran in a rebuke to Trump
items.summary_raw: Four Republicans joined Democrats to approve the measure. And while it is not legally binding, the resolution represents growing frustration inside Congress over the war.

items.title: Questions remain after fatal police shooting in Madison, Wisconsin
items.summary_raw: Police shot and killed a man who they say was stealing bicycles and checking car doors. The four officers involved have been placed on administrative leave.

items.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
items.summary_raw: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region.

items.title: Popular childhood drinks may raise blood pressure decades later
items.summary_raw: A 25-year study found that frequent consumption of soda, sports drinks, and fruit juice beginning in childhood was linked to a higher risk of high blood pressure in adulthood. Swapping one daily drink for whole fruit, milk, or water could significantly reduce that risk.

items.summary_raw: The US president says the landmark agreement to help Saudis develop nuclear energy is dependent on it joining the Abraham Accords.

items.title: Antarctica may give the world decades to prepare for rising seas
items.summary_raw: Scientists may be able to reliably forecast Antarctic ice loss through the middle of this century, offering governments a vital head start on sea level rise. After that, unstable ice retreat could accelerate dramatically, making the future much harder to predict.

items.title: Along Lake Superior, erosion threatens the dead and the living's memories
items.summary_raw: Low lying cemeteries near shorelines face threats of erosion as water levels rise. That is especially true for two cemeteries near Lake Superior, the largest of the country's Great Lakes.

items.title: Ukraine's ousted defence minister insists on being reinstated
items.summary_raw: Mykhailo Fedorov refuses President Zelensky's offer to accept an alternative post, as the battle of wills continues.

items.title: NASA will test SpaceX and Blue Origin Moon landers before the next lunar landing
items.summary_raw: NASA is preparing for future astronaut landings on the Moon with an unusually ambitious orbital rehearsal. During Artemis III in 2027, Orion astronauts will rendezvous and dock separately with prototype lunar landers from Blue Origin and SpaceX, allowing crews and ground teams to test the complex maneuvers needed for later missions.

items.title: NASA’s Webb telescope discovers giant planet hiding in one of astronomy’s most famous systems
items.summary_raw: A giant planet had been hiding inside one of astronomy’s most closely studied planetary systems, concealed by a bright disk of cosmic dust. Webb revealed Beta Pictoris d through atmospheric traces of carbon monoxide, water vapor, and methane, showcasing a promising new method for finding elusive exoplanets.

items.title: Raleigh Housing Authority seeks Cary approval to buy 340-unit apartment complex
items.summary_raw: The Raleigh Housing Authority is seeking Cary’s approval to buy Amberwood at Lochmere and preserve all 340 apartments as affordable housing for at least 30 years. The deal would prevent potential rent hikes but would likely cost Cary and Wake County about $219,000 a year in property-tax revenue.

items.title: The Trump Administration Wants to Change Public Engagement Rules for Polluters
items.summary_raw: The EPA has proposed a rule change that would let states decide how much input the public has on air pollution permits. It would be a huge boon for data centers increasingly reliant on fossil fuels.

items.title: Goodbye, cavities? New gel could regrow tooth enamel
items.summary_raw: Researchers have developed a fluoride-free gel that could help rebuild damaged tooth enamel, something the body cannot naturally replace once it is lost. Inspired by the proteins that guide enamel formation in infancy, the gel fills microscopic cracks and draws calcium and phosphate from saliva to grow a new mineral layer that closely matches healthy enamel.

items.summary_raw: Republicans passed more than $1 trillion for the Pentagon alongside a budget blueprint to fund the war with Iran and implement provisions of President Trump's election overhaul bill.

items.summary_raw: Mamadou Djouma Bah, 17, was arrested and sentenced to prison for exam fraud earlier this month.

items.title: Natural compound may fight rheumatoid arthritis at its source
items.summary_raw: A plant-derived compound reduced swelling, inflammation, and joint damage in rats with rheumatoid arthritis. It also helped rebalance immune activity and improve the way certain fats were processed in the body. Researchers say the findings could lead to a new approach for treating the disease.

items.title: Police take 16-year-old into custody in connection to Glenwood South shooting on July 4
items.summary_raw: Police took an unnamed 16-year-old into custody on Wednesday after one of the July 4th shootings in Glenwood South. The teen is facing several charges and is being held at a juvenile detention facility.

items.summary_raw: The agreement gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.

items.title: Amid a big hiring push, police experts question ICE officer vetting
items.summary_raw: The recent fatal shootings by ICE officers in Texas and Maine come at a time when the federal agency has hired a huge number of officers quickly. Some law enforcement experts worry that this kind of a hiring spree can lead police agencies to cut corners in vetting new hires.

items.title: Ex-governor convicted of murdering a pregnant student in Kenya
items.summary_raw: Okoth Obado was found guilty of killing Sharon Otieno with whom he had had an affair.

items.title: Scientists discover the hidden brain switch that assigns bees their jobs
items.summary_raw: Researchers found that specific brain circuits help decide which jobs bees perform as they age. When those circuits were switched off, older worker bees began caring for the queen again instead of carrying out their normal later-life duties. The findings reveal that a bee colony’s complex division of labor may be guided by neural switches hidden inside each bee’s brain.

items.title: Crashes, closures and flooding across central NC from heavy rain
items.summary_raw: Heavy rain is causing flooding, crashes, downed trees and thousands of power outages across central North Carolina, with more rain expected through Thursday evening.

items.summary_raw: MAGA-coded adult content playing off immigration crackdowns, white supremacy, and obeying the “natural order” is finding rabid audiences on X.

items.summary_raw: The move comes days after President Daniel Ortega demanded 'a wall, a blockade' against opposition parties.

items.title: Ancient ferns turned Triassic Europe into a wildfire inferno
items.summary_raw: Ancient fern savannahs may have transformed parts of Europe into a wildfire-prone inferno during the end-Triassic mass extinction. New fossil evidence suggests the ferns repeatedly regrew after fires, creating fresh fuel that helped prolong the devastation.

items.title: Gas leak prompts dozens of evacuations near South Roxboro Street in Durham
items.summary_raw: Authorities say 30 people were evacuated after a gas leak early Thursday morning.

items.title: Colorectal cancer warning signs younger adults should never ignore
items.summary_raw: Colorectal cancer is now the leading cause of cancer death among people under 50, and cases in younger adults continue to rise. Warning signs can include persistent bowel changes, recurring rectal bleeding, dark or narrow stools, unexplained weight loss, and fatigue. These symptoms often have harmless causes, but they should not be dismissed, especially when they are new or ongoing. Early detection can greatly improve treatment outcomes.

items.title: Duke Energy receives first advanced gas turbine to generate electricity more efficiently
items.summary_raw: The Person County Energy Complex received the first of four new advanced gas turbines, marking an important milestone in Duke Energy's push to modernization.

items.summary_raw: The risks are real. But banning children from the internet could create new problems without solving the ones that really matter.

items.summary_raw: The petition argues the violence against migrants in South Africa could amount to crimes against humanity.

items.title: Lab-grown mini brains may predict which Alzheimer’s treatments will work
items.summary_raw: Miniature brain models grown from patients’ cells revealed striking differences in how Alzheimer’s-related tissue responds to an antidepressant. The organoids and the particles they release could eventually guide more personalized treatments and provide new clues for diagnosing the disease.

items.title: My Week of Transparent Living With Apple’s ‘More Clear’ Liquid Glass Setting
items.summary_raw: I cranked Apple’s iOS 27 beta to maximum transparency on my iPhone and enjoyed every warped second.

items.summary_raw: Signing a nuclear pact in the Middle East is a bold move that has drawn criticism in the US and in Israel.

items.title: A strange quantum effect dramatically boosts energy transfer
items.summary_raw: Researchers discovered a proton-assisted mechanism that greatly improves how triplet energy moves between quantum dots and nearby molecules. The proton briefly shifts position, helps coordinate electron movement, and then returns to where it started. This quantum-driven shuttle could offer a powerful new way to tune solar cells, lasers, and catalytic reactions.

items.title: Remember Jibo? Its Successor Is a Wearable That Turns Your Life Into AI Slop
items.summary_raw: With “blessings” from the original Jibo founders, iKairos is a wearable or desk-mounted “AI journal” that turns your family moments into AI images and video.

items.title: Liquor store employee narrowly escapes leopard attack
items.summary_raw: After nearly five hours, officials captured the animal and it was later released back into the wild, local authorities confirmed.

items.title: MIT’s new lidar chip could give self-driving cars a wider view
items.summary_raw: MIT engineers have found a way to give chip-based lidar a wider, clearer view without relying on moving parts. Their design uses differently shaped antennas that can sit close together without scrambling one another’s signals. In tests, the system sharply reduced interference while steering a single precise beam across a broad field of view.

items.title: ‘The Child Is Terrified’: Measles Doctors Speak Out
items.summary_raw: Hear from six pediatricians in measles hot spot Utah, as the US faces infection levels not seen in over three decades and prepares to lose its status as a country that eliminated the dangerous disease.

items.title: 'Let's enjoy the ride' says Elon Musk, as AI fears mount
items.summary_raw: Talking to The Economist, Musk said he doesn't care if people hate him and said he was not a racist.
